mnemonics: Most Ordinary Linear Recurrences Come In Second Order
Never Forget The Exponent And The Constant
keyPoints: A first order linear recurrence relation is of the form: $a_n = ra_{n-1} + c$
where $a_n$ is the nth term, $r$ is the common ratio, and $c$ is the constant term.
The solution to the recurrence relation can be found using the formula: $a_n = c + (c-r)r^{n-1}$
